message:
test(pager): add TRACK_Q (bd-aztlm) flat hash page cache test suite

Six targeted regression tests plus shared helpers for the
`FlatPageSlots` flat-hash page cache implementation, tracked under
beads id `bd-aztlm` with the `TRACK_Q:` log prefix so runs can be
scraped from the test output.

## Shared helpers (new)

- `emit_track_q_log` — structured JSON log line (`TRACK_Q:{...}`) that
  captures phase, elapsed_ns, page count, bucket/chain/resize counters,
  cache hit rate, and a freeform `extra` payload. Used by every new
  test to produce a uniform machine-readable trace.
- `track_q_page_buf` — deterministic `PageBuf` factory that stamps the
  little-endian page number into the first 4 bytes and fills the rest
  with `page_pattern(page_no)`, giving both insert-side content and a
  cheap end-to-end validator.
- `assert_track_q_page` — verifies both the header (page number) and
  tail byte (pattern) of a read-back page so a truncated or corrupted
  slot is caught immediately.
- `track_q_probe_distance` — given a `FlatPageSlots` and a page number
  already in the table, returns the number of probe steps from the
  ideal bucket, used by the collision chain test.
- `track_q_collision_pages` — walks the u32 page-number space to find
  `wanted` page numbers that hash to the same target bucket so the
  chain test can guarantee probe collisions rather than relying on
  statistical luck.
- `track_q_hit_rate` — saturating hit-rate calculator for the log.

## Tests (new)

1. `test_track_q_flat_hash_basic_insert_get_on_100_pages` — baseline
   insert/get of 100 pages into a 128-slot table. Verifies every page
   is newly inserted and readable.
2. `test_track_q_flat_hash_forced_probe_collision_chain` — uses
   `track_q_collision_pages` to deliberately load N pages into a
   single starting bucket and verifies that probe distances grow
   monotonically and that every page is still reachable.
3. `test_track_q_flat_hash_capacity_growth_uses_overflow_shards_without_resize`
   — asserts the cache routes overflow into shards rather than
   triggering an in-place resize, confirming the "no resize under
   normal growth" contract.
4. `test_track_q_flat_hash_remove_and_reclaim_tombstone_slots` —
   insert / remove / re-insert cycle that verifies tombstone slots are
   reclaimed so that the table does not monotonically grow under churn.
5. `test_track_q_flat_hash_latency_hot_probe_sub_15ns` — micro-bench
   assertion that a hot cache hit on the flat path stays under 15ns,
   catching regressions that would push the fast path back onto the
   chained fallback.
6. `test_track_q_flat_hash_concurrent_reads_eight_threads` — 8-thread
   concurrent read sanity check, verifying that the lock-free read
   path returns consistent data under contention.

All tests are additive — no production code in `page_cache.rs`
changes. The `BEAD_TRACK_Q` constant joins the existing
`BEAD_ID`/`BEAD_TRACK_F` constants at the top of the `mod tests`
block.
